prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response:
A brave stranger, willing to be told they might be wrong!

Here's my choice: I'm going to take a guess that you probably think that humans only use 10% of their brains. This is a common myth that has been debunked by experts in neuroscience and psychology. In reality, brain imaging techniques have shown that different areas of the brain are active at different times, and even the supposedly "idle" areas are still performing important background functions, like regulating the body's automatic systems. So, it's unlikely that 90% of the brain is just sitting idle!
